item.title: EasyJet in talks with Castlelake after rejecting £4.9bn takeover offer
item.description: Budget airline turned down offer that US private credit group submitted on Tuesday

item.description: Abbas Sajwani, son of the ‘Donald of Dubai’, set to make one of the UK’s most expensive purchases

item.title: FTAV’s further reading
item.description: Mystery retatrutide user; the invention of Antifa; Hollywood vs Big Tech; Haaland is good at scoring goals

item.title: Micron posts 15-fold profit surge in boost for global AI stocks
item.description: Chipmaker forecasts sustained demand for computer memory, boosting its shares and Asian markets

item.title: Ari Emanuel eyes Broadway and the West End
item.description: The Hollywood power broker is in advanced talks to buy theatre group ATG Entertainment for £4.5bn

item.title: Hospitals cancel operations owing to lack of air conditioning
item.description: Record June temperatures putting up to 4,000 surgeries at risk

item.title: Patent cliffs aren’t the only driver of the biotech M&A boom
item.description: Many deals this year have been for early-stage assets

item.title: Boaz Weinstein is half right on Workspace’s valuation rut
item.description: London’s commercial property market is lumpy and slowing

item.title: Quinn Emanuel takes charge of £36bn BHP case from troubled boutique
item.description: US law firm now leading UK’s biggest class action after lawyers resigned from Pogust Goodhead

item.title: Rome airports warn of summer ‘disaster’ unless EU border controls suspended
item.description: Biometric checks must be put on hold to cope with passenger numbers, airports boss says

item.title: The future of TV: lots of choice, lots of hassle
item.description: The fracturing of platforms has created both delight and frustration for viewers

item.title: Shareholders push Ocado board to oust CEO Tim Steiner
item.description: Co-founder could remain at grocery tech company in a different role

item.title: Insurers slash war premiums for Strait of Hormuz ships
item.description: Premiums for hull war coverage drop by more than half as ceasefire deal holds

item.title: Q&A: What the Isa shake-up means for first-time buyers and investors
item.description: A raft of rule changes will apply to the popular tax-free savings and investment products

item.title: Aircon stocks soar on record heat and data centre demand
item.description: Rising temperatures drive sales but consumers may be priced out of market by AI industry build-out

item.title: What if ideas aren’t getting harder to find, after all?
item.description: Large companies may have just become better at hiding the fruits of their labour

item.title: NHS England admits key data does not prove Palantir’s effectiveness
item.description: Health service adds caveat saying that performance improvements cannot be attributed to £330mn contract

item.title: Meta races to replace human moderation with AI
item.description: Facebook parent is accelerating plans to use large language models to review content and ads across its platforms

item.title: What are the legal considerations for a destination wedding?
item.description: We want to know about the potential pitfalls of marrying abroad

item.title: Wear in the World: the diamond bag charms loved by the Dubai jet-set
item.description: Bag Bijoux’s designs are created specifically to be worn on the leather straps of Hermès Birkin and Kelly bags

item.title: Snow polo, honeypots and private jets: banks race to woo the wealthy
item.description: Private lenders jostle to win UK’s rich customers who demand more than the traditional branded cheque book and pen

item.title: Stalled subsea cables threaten Middle East digital ambitions
item.description: Enduring suspensions risk denting confidence in region as destination for AI and cloud investment

item.title: Tesco Mobile explores getting back into broadband
item.description: Move by UK’s largest mobile virtual network operator would add further competition to highly fragmented market

item.title: The profits boom is a mixed blessing
item.description: The Kalecki-Levy equation suggests some awkward trade-offs for the US government and markets

item.title: Oil price back at prewar levels as Gulf flows pick up
item.description: Brent crude drops below the $72.48 a barrel it traded at in late February before Iran conflict erupted

item.title: Robot nation: China’s bid to beat its demographic decline
item.description: The country’s workforce is set to fall to 300mn by the end of the century. Beijing wants humanoids to narrow the labour gap

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in the Real Economy: A Visual Guide
item.description: A new series of visual explainers looking at how AI is transforming different industries

item.title: What Ambani’s deep-tech ambitions reveal about India’s AI limits
item.description: Country still needs to play catch-up if it is to become self-reliant in the use of the technology

item.title: FirstFT: Anthropic says Alibaba ‘illicitly’ accessed Claude
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, China detains two Japanese nationals on smuggling charges, and Venezuela to reveal $240bn debt pile
